universe: atlas-only (14,033 tickers: ATP_MAIN 4137 / WTA_MAIN 3683 / ATP_CHALL 5326 / WTA_CHALL 887)
methodology: For each N x (placement_minute in {240,180,120,90,60,40}, bid_offset in {1,2,3,5,8,10,15c}): bid_price = anchor_price_cents - bid_offset (clamp >=1). Walk premarket_tape_v1 forward from placement_minute to T-20m; fill at first minute where price_close <= bid_price OR yes_ask_close <= bid_price (NaN treated as non-filling on that channel). Pure fill mechanics: NO PnL, NO exit logic, NO settlement, NO atlas reproduction. expected_improvement_cents = fill_rate x bid_offset.

path_b_per_regime_fill_summary_v1.parque.note: category x anchor_regime x placement_minute x bid_offset (4x9x6x7)

validation_gates.gate2_coverage: PASS. per-category N exact (ATP_MAIN 4137 / WTA_MAIN 3683 / ATP_CHALL 5326 / WTA_CHALL 887); all 4 categories, all 9 standard anchor regimes present.
validation_gates.gate3_fillrate_low_end: PASS (majority). (P=240, off=1) fill rates 0.65-0.96; 19/36 cells >=0.85, all high. Sub-0.85 cells are underdog/mid regimes -- the drift asymmetry the prompt's Step 1 anticipated (a bid below anchor fills less when the market lands AT anchor; underdog books drift down to anchor). Not a logic error.
validation_gates.gate4_fillrate_high_end: PASS (majority). (P=40, off=15) fill rates 0.00-0.25; 31/36 cells <0.20. The few >0.20 are favorite extremes (r75_84/r85_94) where ~11c upward drift makes a 15c discount occasionally reachable -- correct behavior.
validation_gates.gate5_monotonicity: PASS. fill_rate monotonically decreasing in bid_offset within (category x anchor_regime x placement_minute): 216/216 = 100% (>=80% required). Strongly corroborates simulation correctness.

headline_findings.best_single_uniform_cell: T-240 placement, 15c offset: fill 0.150, expected_improvement 2.25 c/N blended
headline_findings.favorite_regime_optimal: r85_94: place T-180/T-240, 15c offset, fill ~0.42-0.47, expected_improvement ~6.3-7.0 c/N (favorite books drift up ~11c so large offsets fill and capture full offset)
headline_findings.underdog_regime_optimal: r05_14: place T-240, 2-3c offset, fill ~0.39-0.56, expected_improvement ~1.0-1.2 c/N (underdog books drift down to anchor; bids below anchor rarely fill at large offsets)
headline_findings.pattern: expected_improvement and optimal bid_offset both rise monotonically with anchor regime; earlier placement weakly dominates (more window); offset choice dominates placement timing.

deviations: Reframe (per operator direction): the original Path B draft computed hold-to-settlement PnL and compared it against the atlas's +8.70% SPIKE-EXIT baseline -- a conflation (recon measured the hold-to-settlement taker baseline at -1.02c/N, not +8.70%, because you own both legs of each paired event so anchors sum to ~101.9c). This version is PURE FILL MECHANICS: no PnL, no exit logic, no atlas reproduction. expected_improvement is an entry-side edge additive on top of the locked atlas exit.

Gate 3/4 thresholds ('>85% most' / '<20% most') are met in the majority sense but with explainable exceedances reflecting the (prompt-anticipated) favorite/underdog drift asymmetry, not logic errors; gate 5's 100% monotonicity corroborates correctness.
Internal: .to_numpy() returned read-only views; used copy=True for in-place inf-fill of NaN ask/price. No output-contract effect.
